name: Pico de Urbión and the Source of the Duero River Loop
name_original: Sierra al Pico Urbión, Picacho del Camperón, Peñas Blancas y Nacimiento del Río Duero– circular por el Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión
description: Hike a moderate 5.1-mile (8.2 km) loop through glacial landscapes, forests, and peaks in Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de U
region_intro: Hiking around Duruelo De La Sierra offers diverse natural landscapes within the "Green Soria" region, characterized by extensive pine and oak forests, forming the largest wooded area on the Iberian Peninsula. The area is dominated by the Sierra de Urbión mountain range, featuring peaks like Pico de Urbión and the source of the Duero River. Unique geological formations, such as the "enchanted city" of Castroviejo with its sculpted rocks and waterfalls, add to the varied terrain. This environment provides a range of hiking opportunities from verdant forests to rugged mountain paths. Best hiking trails around Duruelo De La Sierra The most popular hiking route is Pico de Urbión and the Source of the Duero River Loop, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route ascends to Pico de Urbión and visits the source of the Duero River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Climb Pico de Urbion — circular through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space, a difficult 8.9 miles (14.4 km) path. This challenging circular route offers expansive views across the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space. Local hikers also love the Route of the Waterfalls of Duruelo de la Sierra, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through scenic cascades, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Hiking around Duruelo De La Sierra is defined by dense pine forests, the rugged Sierra de Urbión mountain range, and unique rock formations. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Duruelo De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Duruelo De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Duruelo De La Sierra?
A: Duruelo De La Sierra offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 20 routes available. These trails cater to different fitness levels, from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Duruelo De La Sierra?
A: The terrain around Duruelo De La Sierra is diverse, characterized by dense pine and oak forests, rugged mountain paths within the Sierra de Urbión, and unique geological formations like the sculpted rocks of Castroviejo. You'll find everything from verdant woodlands to rocky ascents.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy hiking options. For a gentle walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063191511/puente-de-santo-domingo-y-puente-de-los-arrieros-ida-y-vuelta-desde-duruelo-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Santo Domingo y Puente de los Arrieros&lt;/a&gt; route, which is an out-and-back trail from Duruelo de la Sierra. The region has 3 easy routes in total, perfect for those looking for less strenuous activity.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in the area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the area offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041531180/climb-pico-de-urbion-circular-through-the-sierra-de-la-demanda-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb Pico de Urbion — circular through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 14.4 km path provides expansive views and a significant ascent. There are 7 difficult routes in total for those looking to push their limits.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, Duruelo De La Sierra is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062312233/route-of-the-waterfalls-of-duruelo-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Waterfalls of Duruelo de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, leading you through scenic cascades. You can also visit the Cueva Serena waterfall as part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063201453/mirador-de-castroviejo-cascada-de-la-chorla-y-cueva-serena-circular-desde-duruelo-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Castroviejo, cascada de la Chorla y Cueva Serena&lt;/a&gt; circular route.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I explore on the trails?
A: Hikers can explore several significant natural landmarks. A highlight is the ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt; and a visit to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3328794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Duero River&lt;/a&gt;. The unique rock formations of Castroviejo, often called an 'enchanted city,' also offer impressive viewpoints. Additionally, you can discover several beautiful lakes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6224900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Negra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Duruelo De La Sierra are circular, allowing you to experience diverse landscapes without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058968468/pico-de-urbion-and-the-source-of-the-duero-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión and the Source of the Duero River Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063201453/mirador-de-castroviejo-cascada-de-la-chorla-y-cueva-serena-circular-desde-duruelo-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Castroviejo, cascada de la Chorla y Cueva Serena&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Duruelo De La Sierra?
A: The 'Green Soria' region is beautiful year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, ideal for forest walks. Summer is also popular, especially for higher altitude trails. During winter, the northern parts can become snowy, opening up possibilities for snow-related activities, but some trails may require specific gear or be less accessible.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Duruelo De La Sierra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. Reviewers often praise the extensive pine and oak forests, the stunning natural features like Pico de Urbión and the Source of the Duero River, and the well-marked trails that cater to various ability levels.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Duruelo De La Sierra?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in natural areas like Duruelo De La Sierra. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations or local signage for any restrictions before you go.

Q: Are there any adventurous activities besides hiking in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking more adventure, Duruelo de la Sierra offers the Vía Ferrata Cuerda la Graja. Located near Castroviejo and Cueva Serena, this route combines elements of mountaineering, hiking, and climbing with hanging bridges and a zip line, providing an exciting outdoor experience without requiring expert climbing skills.
tips: The shelter itself is extremely abandoned and I would avoid entering at all costs but the place it is in is impressive.

Cresting by the Urbión Peaks, at 2,101 masl we find ourselves in the Picacho del Camperón with magnificent panoramic views of the places that surround us. At the top, a curiosity awaits us, an ice ax nailed to a rock in the style of the Sword of Excalibur.

Pico de Urbión is a mountain located in the Sierra de Urbión, on the border between the provinces of Soria and La Rioja. It is a popular destination for nature lovers and hikers.
The natural beauty of the Sierra de Urbión offers excellent opportunities for photography.

faqs.question: What is the best starting point for this circular trail?
faqs.answer: Popular starting points for this route include the Laguna Negra itself, or the "bunker" parking area near Castroviejo and Duruelo de la Sierra. The "bunker" parking offers convenient access to the source of the Duero River and the ascent to Pico de Urbión.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered moderate to hard. While some sections, particularly from the "bunker" parking towards the Duero source, are moderate, the ascent to Pico de Urbión is a constant and strenuous climb. It requires very good physical fitness, sure-footedness, sturdy shoes, and some alpine experience for certain parts.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse and rugged landscape. The trail passes through dense forests of Scots pine and beech, transitions to high mountain pastures, and features rocky formations. Expect unpaved paths, alpine hiking paths, and mountain hiking paths, all shaped by impressive glacial erosion.

faqs.question: What are the main natural highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: The route showcases several stunning features, including the mysterious Laguna Negra, Laguna Larga, Laguna Helada, and Laguna de Urbión. You'll also visit prominent peaks like Picacho del Camperón (2,101 m) and Pico de Urbión, and the significant Nacimiento del Río Duero, where the Duero River begins its journey.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within the Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or seasonal restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
faqs.answer: There is typically no entrance fee to access the Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión itself. However, during peak seasons, there might be fees for parking or shuttle services to access popular areas like Laguna Negra. Always check local regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is more stable, and snow has usually melted from the higher elevations. Winter hiking is possible but requires specialist equipment and experience due to snow and ice conditions.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Etapa 01: Fuentes del Duero – Vinuesa, the Valle de Urbión, Covaleda-Pico urbión, Sendero del Portilla, and the Sendero del Valle de Urbión.

faqs.question: Are there any shelters or huts along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by El Búnker Shelter, which can offer a basic refuge during your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's moderate to hard difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring sturdy hiking boots, appropriate layers of clothing for changing mountain weather, plenty of water and snacks, a map and compass or GPS device, and sun protection. Trekking poles can also be very helpful, especially on descents.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints offering panoramic views?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. From Pico de Urbión (2,228 meters), the highest point in the Sierra de Urbión, you are rewarded with spectacular panoramic views of the surrounding mountains, glacial cirques, and the vast landscape of Soria province.
